aiCare.title: AI Care
aiCare.greeting: Hi Kiran 👋 I'm your AI health companion. I know your records, so I can explain your reports, your medicines, or help you book a visit — in plain language. What would you like to know?
aiCare.fallback: I can help with your reports, medicines, symptoms, appointments and bills. Try a suggestion, or ask in your own words — English or हिंदी.
aiCare.replyReport: Your latest CBC is mostly normal — haemoglobin 13.2 g/dL is healthy. Your white-cell count is slightly raised, which usually means your body is fighting a minor infection. Your doctor has already seen it.
aiCare.replySerious: Because you reported chest tightness with breathlessness, you've been marked high priority and fast-tracked. Please stay on the premises — if it worsens, tell any staff member immediately or use the Emergency button.
aiCare.replyMedicine: You're on 2 medicines. One controls blood sugar (take with food), one is a short course for your current symptoms. I checked — no harmful interactions. I'll remind you when each dose is due.
aiCare.replyBook: Your earliest good slot is Thursday 4:00 PM with Dr. Priya Nair — the shortest wait that day. Want me to book it?
aiCare.modelNote: Model: agentix-ai-v1 · Processed under DISHA. AI never makes a final clinical decision — it assists your care team. Manage access in Profile & Privacy.
aiCare.decTriageDetail: AI assessed your symptoms as High priority and fast-tracked you.
aiCare.decBriefDetail: AI summarised your history & symptoms for your doctor before the visit.
aiCare.decLabDetail: AI flagged a slightly raised WBC and explained it in plain language.
aiCare.decDietDetail: AI drafted a diabetic-friendly diet plan for your review.

assistant.title: AI Health Assistant
assistant.subtitle: Private to you · explains your records in plain language · English / हिंदी
assistant.greeting: Hi Kiran 👋 I'm your AI health companion. I know your records, so I can explain your reports, your medicines, or help you book a visit — in plain language. What would you like to know?
assistant.fallback: I can help with your reports, medicines, symptoms, appointments and bills. Try one of the suggestions below, or ask in your own words — English or हिंदी.
assistant.replyReport: Your latest CBC is mostly normal — haemoglobin 13.2 g/dL is healthy. Your white-cell count is slightly raised, which usually means your body is fighting a minor infection. Nothing alarming, and your doctor has already seen it.
assistant.replySerious: Because you reported chest tightness with breathlessness, you've been marked high priority and fast-tracked. Please stay on the premises — if the tightness gets worse or spreads to your arm/jaw, tell any staff member immediately or press the Emergency button.
assistant.replyMedicine: You're on 2 medicines right now. One controls blood sugar (take with food), one is a short course for your current symptoms. I checked — there are no harmful interactions between them. I'll remind you when each dose is due.
assistant.replyBook: Your earliest good slot is Thursday 4:00 PM with Dr. Priya Nair — that's the shortest wait that day. Want me to book it for you?
assistant.replyDiet: Based on your diagnosis I've prepared a diabetic-friendly plan: ~1800 kcal/day, high fibre, low simple sugars. I can show it in Hindi too. Open 'Care & Follow-up' to see the full plan.

discharge.collectFrom: Collect from Pharmacy counter (Ground Floor) before leaving.

emergency.title: Emergency Visits
emergency.subtitle: Live status from the ER team · plain-language summary of your care
emergency.portalOnlyBody: Sign in as a patient to view your emergency visits.
emergency.noVisitsBody: You don't have any emergency visits in your record. If you are unwell now and need urgent help, call 102 or visit our 24×7 ER directly.
emergency.news2High: Your readings need urgent attention from a doctor. The team is monitoring closely.
emergency.news2Medium: Your readings are slightly off and the team is checking you more often than usual.
emergency.news2Low: Your readings look stable. The team will keep monitoring as normal.

familyTrack.invalidLink: This tracking link is invalid or consent has not been granted.
familyTrack.statusNotAvailable: Status update not yet available. Please check back shortly.
familyTrack.cameraHint: View the hospital room camera feed — requires nurse approval
familyTrack.nurseWillEnable: A nurse will enable the camera shortly
familyTrack.declinedBody: The nurse has declined the camera request at this time
familyTrack.poweredBy: Powered by Agentix HIMS · DISHA Compliant
familyTrack.privacyBanner: This page shows non-clinical journey information only. No medical data is shared.

feedback.title: Feedback & Experience
feedback.subtitle: Your feedback helps us improve care for everyone.
feedback.noRequestsBody: After each visit, a feedback request will appear here.
feedback.npsQuestion: How likely are you to recommend us?
feedback.recommendQuestion: Would you recommend us to family or friends?
feedback.thankYou: Thank you — your feedback has been recorded

followup.title: Care & Follow-up
followup.subtitle: Your recovery plan after this visit
followup.aiSummaryReviewed: AI summary · reviewed by your doctor
followup.visitSummaryBody: You came in with fever and fatigue. Your doctor examined you, ordered a few tests to be safe, and started you on medicines to manage your blood sugar and blood pressure. Most likely this is a minor infection. Take your medicines on time, watch for the symptoms below, and come back for your follow-up.
followup.medScheduleEmpty: Your medicine schedule will appear here once you review and accept your doctor's orders.
followup.tapSymptom: Tap any symptom you're experiencing right now:
followup.guidanceEmergency: This may be a medical emergency. Call 102 or go to the nearest emergency room immediately.
followup.guidanceContact: Please contact your care team. If symptoms worsen, visit the hospital.
followup.redFlag1: High fever above 39°C for more than 2 days
followup.redFlag3: Persistent vomiting — unable to keep fluids down
followup.dietTip1: Low-sugar, low-salt balanced meals · small and frequent
followup.dietTip2: Drink 2–3 litres of water through the day
followup.dietTip4: Take medicines on time — set the reminders below

healthStory.title: My Health Story
healthStory.subtitle: An AI-curated view of your health over time
healthStory.aiSummaryBody: Over the last 6 months your blood pressure has steadily improved (from 142/92 to 130/85) — your medication and lifestyle changes are working. Your diabetes is borderline and due for a 3-month check. Today's visit is for new chest symptoms, being treated as high priority. Keep up the BP routine and book your diabetes follow-up.

help.title: Help & Emergency
help.subtitle: Get help fast — anytime during your care
help.emergencyBody: If this is a medical emergency, get help immediately.
help.faq1Q: How do I know when it's my turn?
help.faq1A: Your dashboard updates live and you'll get a notification (and WhatsApp) the moment you're called — with the exact room.
help.faq2Q: Can my family see my status?
help.faq2A: Yes, if you've given consent. They get a private link showing non-clinical status only — no diagnoses or results.
help.faq3Q: Is the AI making decisions about me?
help.faq3A: No. AI only assists — every clinical decision is made and approved by your doctor. See "AI in my Care" for full transparency.

insurance.title: Insurance & Claims
insurance.subtitle: Your cashless coverage and claim status
insurance.noClaimBody: Once a claim is initiated by the insurance desk, it will appear here.
insurance.denialRiskHint: Tap "Run AI analysis" to estimate the likelihood of TPA denial and the levers to reduce it.
insurance.eachDocLowers: Each verified document lowers your denial risk and accelerates the claim.

ipd.notAdmittedBody: If you're admitted to a ward, your live care plan, doctor rounds and recovery will appear here.

ipd.sgInOt: You're in the operation theatre now — your family will be kept updated.

orders.youDecideBody: Accept what you need, reduce the quantity of anything you already have at home, or skip an item with a reason. You only pay for what you keep.
